{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM docker:windowsservercore-ltsc2025

# Install .NET 9 SDK using the official dotnet-install script
RUN powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command \
    $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'; \
    Invoke-WebRequest -OutFile dotnet-install.ps1 https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.ps1; \
    & '.\dotnet-install.ps1' -Channel 9.0 -InstallDir 'C:\dotnet'; \
    [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable('PATH', [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('PATH', 'Machine') + ';C:\\dotnet', 'Machine')

ENV PATH=C:\\dotnet;${PATH}

WORKDIR /src

# Build arguments (can be overridden at build time)
ARG SOLUTION_FILE_PATH=ShareX.sln
ARG RUNTIME_ID=win-x64
ARG PLATFORM=x64
ARG CONFIGURATION=Release

# Copy source code
COPY . .

# Restore and build
RUN dotnet restore --runtime "${RUNTIME_ID}" "${SOLUTION_FILE_PATH}"
RUN dotnet build --no-restore --configuration "${CONFIGURATION}" -p:Platform="${PLATFORM}" --self-contained true /m:1 "${SOLUTION_FILE_PATH}"

# Deploy built artifacts to a clean folder for runtime use
RUN powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command \
    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path 'C:\\ShareX' -Force; \
    Copy-Item -Path ".\\ShareX\\bin\\${CONFIGURATION}" -Destination 'C:\\ShareX' -Recurse -Force

# Default entrypoint: try to locate and run the first executable found under C:\ShareX
ENTRYPOINT ["powershell", "-NoProfile", "-ExecutionPolicy", "Bypass", "-Command", "try { $exe = Get-ChildItem -Path 'C:\\ShareX' -Filter *.exe -Recurse | Sort-Object FullName | Select-Object -First 1; if ($exe) { & $exe.FullName } else { Write-Error 'No executable found'; exit 1 } } catch { exit 1 }"]
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM mcr.microsoft.com/windows/servercore:ltsc2022 AS build
SHELL ["powershell", "-NoProfile", "-ExecutionPolicy", "Bypass"]

# Install .NET 9 SDK using the dotnet-install script
RUN $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'; \
    Invoke-WebRequest -OutFile dotnet-install.ps1 https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.ps1; \
    & .\dotnet-install.ps1 -Channel 9.0 -InstallDir 'C:\\dotnet'; \
    [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable('PATH', [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('PATH','Machine') + ';C:\\dotnet', 'Machine')

ENV PATH=C:\\dotnet;${PATH}

WORKDIR C:\\src

# Copy source code
COPY . .

# Build arguments (defaults, can be overridden at build time)
ARG SOLUTION_FILE_PATH=ShareX.sln
ARG RUNTIME_ID=win-x64
ARG PLATFORM=x64
ARG CONFIGURATION=Release

# Restore and build
RUN dotnet restore --runtime "${RUNTIME_ID}" "${SOLUTION_FILE_PATH}"
RUN dotnet build --no-restore --configuration "${CONFIGURATION}" -p:Platform="${PLATFORM}" --self-contained true /m:1 "${SOLUTION_FILE_PATH}"

# Stage 2: final runtime image containing built artifacts
FROM mcr.microsoft.com/windows/servercore:ltsc2022 AS runtime
WORKDIR C:\\src

# Copy built artifacts from the build stage
COPY --from=build C:\\src\\bin C:\\ShareX\\bin

# Default message on startup (GUI apps typically won't run headless in container)
CMD ["powershell","-NoProfile","-ExecutionPolicy","Bypass","-Command","Write-Host 'ShareX container ready. Artifacts are in C:\\ShareX\\bin'" ]
"}

iterations.injected_lessons:
1. **Base image not found errors**  
   - *What happened:* Most failures (Steps 6, 8, 13, 15, 18–25) returned “Error: base image not found on Docker Hub” for images like  
     - `mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:9.0-windowsservercore-ltsc2022`  
     - `mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:9.0-windowsservercore-ltsc2025`  
     - `mcr.microsoft.com/windows/servercore:ltsc2022`  
   - *Cause:* These specific tags do not exist (or are private/unpublished). Microsoft maintains official .NET Windows images under limited, validated tags.  
   - *Fix:* Use existing public tags verified on MCR or Docker Hub:  
     - `mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:8.0-windowsservercore-ltsc2022` (latest LTS) for build.  
     - `mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/runtime:8.0-windowsservercore-ltsc2022` for runtime.  
     Do not use “9.0” or “ltsc2025” until officially available.

2. **Linux-style syntax in Windows containers**  
   - *What happened:* VerifyBuild rejected Dockerfiles using Linux-style environment expansion (`${PATH}`, `${VAR:-default}`), and POSIX shell assumptions (`sh` commands).  
   - *Fix:* In Windows container stages, use PowerShell syntax and escaping:  
     - Use `$env:PATH` instead of `${PATH}`.  
     - Avoid Bash parameter expansion; use PowerShell’s conditional expressions (`if`, `Test-Path`, etc.).  
     - Ensure all `RUN` lines use PowerShell or `cmd`.

3. **Invalid starter image**  
   - *What happened:* Early use of `FROM docker:windowsservercore-ltsc2025` failed; no such Windows “docker” image exists.  
   - *Fix:* For Windows containers, always start from Microsoft’s official image sources (`mcr.microsoft.com/windows` or `mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/...`).

4. **Testing environment mismatch**  
   - *What happened:* Step 17’s VerifyBuild reported test failures because “smoke tests assume a POSIX shell (sh)” which Windows containers lack.  
   - *Fix:* Adjust tests to use PowerShell commands or omit them for Windows builds. Alternatively switch to Linux-based .NET SDK images if POSIX tooling is required.

5. **Unnecessary version speculation**  
   - *What happened:* The build repeatedly tried “.NET 9 SDK” images that do not yet exist.  
   - *Fix:* Use currently released major version (8.0) and update later when official 9.0 images appear.

6. **Action plan for next attempt**  
   - Use base: `mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:8.0-windowsservercore-ltsc2022`.  
   - Stick to PowerShell syntax (`SHELL ["powershell", "-NoProfile", "-ExecutionPolicy", "Bypass"]`).  
   - Verify with simple PowerShell-based smoke tests.  
   - Do not use nonexistent `ltsc2025` or `.NET 9` tags.

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM mcr.microsoft.com/windows/servercore:ltsc2022 AS build
SHELL ["powershell", "-NoProfile", "-ExecutionPolicy", "Bypass"]

# Working directory in the container
WORKDIR C:\\src

# Install .NET 8 SDK (self-contained) using the official dotnet-install script
ENV SOLUTION_FILE_PATH="C:\\src\\ShareX.sln"
ENV RUNTIME_ID="win-x64"

RUN $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'; 
    # Download the dotnet-install script and install .NET 8 SDK to C:\\dotnet
    Invoke-WebRequest -OutFile dotnet-install.ps1 -Uri 'https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download/dotnet-core/scripts/v1/dotnet-install.ps1'; 
    & .\\dotnet-install.ps1 -Version 8.0.0 -InstallDir C:\\dotnet; 
    [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable('PATH', $env:PATH + ';C:\\dotnet', 'Machine')

# Copy all source code into the container
COPY . .

# Restore and build from source (x64 as per standard CI matrix)
RUN $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'; 
    & C:\\dotnet\\dotnet.exe restore --runtime $env:RUNTIME_ID $env:SOLUTION_FILE_PATH; 
    & C:\\dotnet\\dotnet.exe build --configuration Release -p:Platform="x64" --self-contained true /m:1 $env:SOLUTION_FILE_PATH

# Runtime image containing the built artifacts
FROM mcr.microsoft.com/windows/servercore:ltsc2022 AS runtime
WORKDIR C:\\src

# Copy built artifacts from the build stage
COPY --from=build C:\\src C:\\src

# Default command: indicate where artifacts are located

COPY Directory.build.props Directory.build.props

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM mcr.microsoft.com/windows/servercore:ltsc2022 AS build
SHELL ["powershell", "-NoLogo", "-ExecutionPolicy", "Bypass"]

# Install .NET 9 SDK using official dotnet-install.ps1
RUN $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'; \
    if (-Not (Test-Path 'C:\\dotnet')) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path 'C:\\dotnet' | Out-Null } ; \
    Invoke-WebRequest -UseBasicParsing -Uri 'https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.ps1' -OutFile 'C:\\dotnet-install.ps1' ; \
    & 'C:\\dotnet-install.ps1' -Channel 9.0 -InstallDir 'C:\\dotnet'; \
    [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable('PATH', $Env:PATH + ';C:\\dotnet', 'Machine')

WORKDIR C:\src

# Copy project files
COPY ShareX.sln .
COPY Directory.Build.props ./Directory.Build.props
COPY Directory.Packages.props ./Directory.Packages.props
COPY ShareX/ ShareX/
COPY ShareX.Setup/ ShareX.Setup/

# Build and publish
RUN dotnet restore --runtime win-x64 "ShareX.sln" ; \
    dotnet publish -c Release -r win-x64 -p:Platform=x64 "ShareX/ShareX.csproj" -o Publish\ShareX ; \
    if (Test-Path 'ShareX.Setup/ShareX.Setup.csproj') { dotnet publish -c Release -r win-x64 "ShareX.Setup/ShareX.Setup.csproj" -o Publish\ShareX.Setup } else { Write-Host 'No Setup project' }

FROM mcr.microsoft.com/windows/servercore:ltsc2022
WORKDIR C:\app

# Copy built artifacts from build stage
COPY --from=build C:\src\Publish\ShareX C:\app\ShareX
COPY --from=build C:\src\Publish\ShareX.Setup C:\app\ShareX.Setup

# Entry point: attempt to run ShareX.exe if present
CMD ["powershell", "-NoProfile", "-Command", "If (Test-Path 'C:\\app\\ShareX\\ShareX.exe') { Start-Process -FilePath 'C:\\app\\ShareX\\ShareX.exe' } else { Write-Host 'ShareX.exe not found' }; If (Test-Path 'C:\\app\\ShareX.Setup\\ShareX.Setup.exe') { Start-Process -FilePath 'C:\\app\\ShareX.Setup\\ShareX.Setup.exe' } ; Start-Sleep -Seconds 3600"]
"}
